title: Scheelea rostrata
taxonomy: Scheelea rostrata is classified as a species of palm tree in the genus Scheelea. It belongs to the family Arecaceae. Scheelea rostrata is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The species is commonly found in countries such as Costa Rica, Panama, and Colombia. Scheelea rostrata is a single-stemmed palm that grows up to 20 meters tall.

intro: Scheelea rostrata, also known as the "Mayan nut," is a species of tree native to the tropical rainforests of Central America, belonging to the palm family (Arecaceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and protect against cell damage.
